pi_items.source: setup_task.sh logic injects degradation on INV-004 (output *= 0.85).
setup_task.sh logic injects outage on INV-002 by setting output to 0.
setup_task.sh logic explicitly applies the outage when 35 <= day <= 39.
setup_task.sh explicitly lists 6 arrays from INV-001 to INV-006.
setup_task.sh assigns 840 kWp capacity to each of the 6 arrays (840 * 6 = 5040).
verifier.py checks for capacity factor numbers exclusively within this [0.15, 0.28] range. The dynamically generated values fall between 0.203 and 0.240, satisfying this verified expectation.
verifier.py checks for performance ratio numbers exclusively within this [0.60, 0.90] range. The dynamically generated values fall between 0.744 and 0.876, satisfying this verified expectation.

verifier.py strictly expects at least one specific yield value within [300, 500]. Although healthy arrays generate a yield up to 518 kWh/kWp, the expected yield for the degraded array (440) and outage array (486) properly trigger the verifier within this range.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated hourly solar data. It is verified that the solar farm has 6 arrays with a total capacity of 5040 kWp. The data correctly injects an outage into array INV-002 on days 35-39, and degradation (output reduced to 85%) into array INV-004. Verifier logic explicitly checks for capacity factor within [0.15, 0.28], performance ratio within [0.60, 0.90], and specific yield within [300, 500]. While healthy arrays generate yields up to 518, the lower yields from the degraded and outage arrays reliably trigger the verifier's [300, 500] range expectation. Thus, all metadata claims perfectly describe the dataset bounds and evaluation criteria.
